content.full_text:
December 24, 2002

James R. Sutton
Nielsen, Merksamer,
Parrinello, Mueller & Naylor, LLP
591 Redwood Highway, Suite 4000
Mill Valley, CA 94941-3039

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-02-317

Dear Mr. Sutton:

This letter is to inform you that we have withdrawn your request for advice on behalf of the San Francisco Coalition for Affordable Public Services ("SFCAPS"). You ask for the same advice previously sought (our file No. W-02-179) in a request withdrawn because, inter alia, you had not identified a client, or referred to a specific set of circumstances underlying your inquiry. Pursuant to Commission policy, we decline to offer the advice you seek since your inquiry relates to the past conduct of this committee insofar as it asks how your firm and SFCAPS should treat fees paid by an unknown third-party. Your request also remains hypothetical in nature, given the absence of any information regarding the third-party payor; any understandings it may have had with the committee, and whether there actually have been or will be any post-election activities or payments to your firm. We have also determined from the records of the Secretary of State that SFCAPS has terminated its existence. (See subdivisions A, D, F and G of regulation 18329(c)(4), copy enclosed.)

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Lawrence T. Woodlock
Senior Counsel, Legal Division
